title: Diamond Model
overview: The Diamond Model is a cybersecurity framework used to analyze intrusions by connecting four interrelated elements: Adversary, Infrastructure, Capability, and Victim. These elements form the atomic structure of any cyberattack, arranged as a diamond to emphasize their relationships.

study.overview: The Diamond Model is a cybersecurity framework used to analyze intrusions by connecting four interrelated elements: Adversary, Infrastructure, Capability, and Victim. These elements form the atomic structure of any cyberattack, arranged as a diamond to emphasize their relationships.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
